68	    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
69	        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.
70	
71	        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
72	        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
73	        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
74	        """
75	        if not self.image:
76	            return self
77

80	            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
81

83	
84	        return self

86	    def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
87	        """Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.
88	
89	        Args:
90	            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes
91	        """
92	        if self.image is not None:
93	            if as_base64:
94	                return BytesIO(self.image)
95	            return BytesIO(base64.b64decode(self.image))
96	        elif self.path is not None:
97	            img_bytes = self.path.read_bytes()
98	            if as_base64:
99	                return BytesIO(base64.b64encode(img_bytes))

104	            img_bytes = response.content
105	            if as_base64:

107	            return BytesIO(img_bytes)
108	        else:

file.content:
def test_chat_message_legacy_roundtrip():
    legacy_message = {
        "role": MessageRole.USER,
        "content": "foo",
        "additional_kwargs": {},
    }
    m = ChatMessage(**legacy_message)
    assert m.model_dump() == {
        "additional_kwargs": {},
        "blocks": [{"block_type": "text", "text": "foo"}],
        "role": MessageRole.USER,
    }


def test_image_block_resolve_image(png_1px: bytes, png_1px_b64: bytes):
    b = ImageBlock(image=png_1px)

    img = b.resolve_image()
    assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
    assert img.read() == png_1px

    img = b.resolve_image(as_base64=True)
    assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
    assert img.read() == png_1px_b64


def test_image_block_resolve_image_path(
    tmp_path: Path, png_1px_b64: bytes, png_1px: bytes
):
    png_path = tmp_path / "test.png"
    png_path.write_bytes(png_1px)

    b = ImageBlock(path=png_path)
    img = b.resolve_image()
    assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
    assert img.read() == png_1px

    img = b.resolve_image(as_base64=True)
    assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
    assert img.read() == png_1px_b64


def test_image_block_resolve_image_url(png_1px_b64: bytes, png_1px: bytes):
    with mock.patch("llama_index.core.base.llms.types.requests") as mocked_req:
        url_str = "http://example.com"
        mocked_req.get.return_value = mock.MagicMock(content=png_1px)
        b = ImageBlock(url=AnyUrl(url=url_str))
        img = b.resolve_image()
        assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
        assert img.read() == png_1px

        img = b.resolve_image(as_base64=True)
        assert isinstance(img, BytesIO)
        assert img.read() == png_1px_b64


def test_image_block_resolve_error():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="No image found in the chat message!"):
        b = ImageBlock()
        b.resolve_image()


def test_image_block_store_as_anyurl():
    url_str = "http://example.com"
    b = ImageBlock(url=url_str)
    assert b.url == AnyUrl(url=url_str)

content.text: Good, `png_1px` is raw bytes and `resolve_image` expects `b.image` to end up base64-encoded. Now let me check the fixture for `png_1px_b64` for the exact format expected.

input.new_string:
def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize raw or already base64-encoded image bytes.

    Returns a tuple of `(encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)`, where
    `encoded_image` is the base64-encoded representation to store and
    `decoded_for_guessing` is the raw bytes to use for mimetype guessing.
    """
    try:
        decoded_for_guessing = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
        encoded_image = image
    except binascii.Error:
        decoded_for_guessing = image
        encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image)

    return encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing


class TextBlock(BaseModel):
    block_type: Literal["text"] = "text"
    text: str

content.text: Both raw bytes and pre-encoded base64 input now normalize correctly and mimetype detection works in both cases (previously, passing already-base64 bytes would have double-encoded and failed mimetype detection). Let me check for messages from agent2 before finalizing.

content.content:
46	
47	def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
48	    """Normalize raw or already base64-encoded image bytes.
49	
50	    Returns a tuple of `(encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)`, where
51	    `encoded_image` is the base64-encoded representation to store and
52	    `decoded_for_guessing` is the raw bytes to use for mimetype guessing.
53	    """
54	    try:
55	        decoded_for_guessing = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
56	        encoded_image = image
57	    except binascii.Error:
58	        decoded_for_guessing = image
59	        encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image)
60	
61	    return encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing
62	
63	
64	class TextBlock(BaseModel):
65	    block_type: Literal["text"] = "text"
66	    text: str
67	
68	
69	class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
70	    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
71	    image: bytes | None = None
72	    path: FilePath | None = None
73	    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
74	    image_mimetype: str | None = None
75	    detail: str | None = None
76	
77	    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
78	    @classmethod
79	    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
80	        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
81	        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
82	            return url
83	        return AnyUrl(url=url)
84	
85	    @model_validator(mode="after")
86	    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
87	        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.
88	
89	        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
90	        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
91	        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
92	        """
93	        if not self.image:
94	            return self
95	
96	        self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
97	
98	        if not self.image_mimetype:
99	            guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing)
100	            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
101	
102	        return self
103	
104	    def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
105	        """Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.
106	
107	        Args:
108	            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes
109	        """
110	        if self.image is not None:
111	            if as_base64:
112	                return BytesIO(self.image)
113	            return BytesIO(base64.b64decode(self.image))
114	        elif self.path is not None:
115	            img_bytes = self.path.read_bytes()
116	            if as_base64:
117	                return BytesIO(base64.b64encode(img_bytes))
118	            return BytesIO(img_bytes)
119	        elif self.url is not None:
120	            # load image from URL
121	            response = requests.get(str(self.url))
122	            img_bytes = response.content
123	            if as_base64:
124	                return BytesIO(base64.b64encode(img_bytes))
125	            return BytesIO(img_bytes)
126	        else:
127	            raise ValueError("No image found in the chat message!")
128	
129	
130	ContentBlock = Annotated[
131	    Union[TextBlock, ImageBlock], Field(discriminator="block_type")
132	]
133	
134	
135	class ChatMessage(BaseModel):
136	    """Chat message."""
137	
138	    role: MessageRole = MessageRole.USER
139	    additional_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
140	    blocks: list[ContentBlock] = Field(default_factory=list)
141	
142	    def __init__(self, /, content: Any | None = None, **data: Any) -> None:
143	        """Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.
144	
145	        If content was passed and contained text, store a single TextBlock.
146	        If content was passed and it was a list, assume it's a list of content blocks and store it.
147	        """
148	        if content is not None:
149	            if isinstance(content, str):
150	                data["blocks"] = [TextBlock(text=content)]
151	            elif isinstance(content, list):
152	                data["blocks"] = content
153	
154	        super().__init__(**data)
155

content.text:
Implemented `normalize_image_bytes` in `llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py`:

- New module-level helper `normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]` that detects whether input bytes are already base64-encoded (via `base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)`), returning `(encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)` in both cases.
- Refactored `ImageBlock.image_to_base64` to call this helper instead of unconditionally re-encoding, and to guess the MIME type from the decoded bytes rather than the encoded ones — fixing a latent bug where already-base64 input would get double-encoded and mimetype guessing would run against encoded bytes.
- Verified against the existing test suite (`llama-index-core/tests/base/llms/test_types.py`, 12 passed) plus a manual check confirming both raw and pre-encoded inputs normalize identically and mimetype detection succeeds in both cases.

Coordinated with agent2 (also editing `image_to_base64` for a `get_image_mimetype()` accessor) via coop-send so they layer their change on top of mine rather than clobbering it. Patch written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.

result:
Implemented `normalize_image_bytes` in `llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py`:

- New module-level helper `normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]` that detects whether input bytes are already base64-encoded (via `base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)`), returning `(encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)` in both cases.
- Refactored `ImageBlock.image_to_base64` to call this helper instead of unconditionally re-encoding, and to guess the MIME type from the decoded bytes rather than the encoded ones — fixing a latent bug where already-base64 input would get double-encoded and mimetype guessing would run against encoded bytes.
- Verified against the existing test suite (`llama-index-core/tests/base/llms/test_types.py`, 12 passed) plus a manual check confirming both raw and pre-encoded inputs normalize identically and mimetype detection succeeds in both cases.

Coordinated with agent2 (also editing `image_to_base64` for a `get_image_mimetype()` accessor) via coop-send so they layer their change on top of mine rather than clobbering it. Patch written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.
